commands.review_summary:
Updates the embedded runner planning-only retry helper and tests so sleep-only bash/exec tool-use turns can retry while ordinary bash tool use remains progress.

PR surface: Source +36, Tests +74. Total +110 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection of current main shows the sleep toolUse path is blocked before planning-only text detection, and the PR body includes terminal proof of the before/after helper behavior.
commands.review_followup: - No ClawSweeper repair lane is needed because the contributor PR already contains the focused source, tests, and real behavior proof.

target.title: fix(agents): detect dummy sleep planning-only turns
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: the strict-agentic planning-only guard treated a `toolUse` terminal turn and any non-`update_plan` tool meta as concrete progress, so a dummy `bash` sleep could bypass the retry.
- Solution: classify `update_plan` plus pure `sleep` `bash`/`exec` metas as no-progress tool activity and continue the planning-only text check for those `toolUse` terminal turns.
- What changed: `resolvePlanningOnlyRetryInstruction()` now distinguishes no-progress tool metas from real progress and keeps ordinary `bash` tool-use turns blocked from planning-only retry.
- What did NOT change: ordinary command execution, file mutations, messaging deliveries, async started tools, and multi-tool progress still suppress planning-only retries.

Fixes #64883.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** A strict-agentic assistant turn that only says it will act and calls `bash` with `sleep 0.1` should still be treated as planning-only; an ordinary `bash` command should not be replayed as planning-only.
- **Real environment tested:** Local Linux checkout in `/home/0668001029/openclaw-worktrees/issue-64883` on branch `feat/issue-64883-planning-tool-progress`, based on `origin/main` at `5483ff705f`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
  ```bash
  python3 - <<'PY'
  import json, subprocess
  path = 'src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/incomplete-turn.ts'
  text = subprocess.check_output(['git', '-C', '/home/0668001029/openclaw-worktrees/issue-64883', 'show', f'origin/main:{path}'], text=True)
  print(json.dumps({
      'originMainStillBlocksToolUseStopReason': 'if (stopReason && stopReason !== "stop")' in text,
      'originMainCountsEveryNonUpdatePlanToolAsProgress': 'entry.toolName !== "update_plan"' in text,
      'originMainHasSleepNoProgressHeuristic': 'PLANNING_ONLY_SLEEP_COMMAND_RE' in text,
  }, indent=2))
  PY

  env -C "/home/0668001029/openclaw-worktrees/issue-64883" corepack pnpm@11.2.2 exec tsx -e 'import { resolvePlanningOnlyRetryInstruction } from "./src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/incomplete-turn.ts"; const base = { provider: "openai", modelId: "gpt-5.4", executionContract: "strict-agentic", prompt: "Please inspect the code, make the change, and run the checks.", aborted: false, timedOut: false }; const attempt = (meta: string) => ({ assistantTexts: ["I\x27ll update the code now."], clientToolCalls: undefined, yieldDetected: false, didSendDeterministicApprovalPrompt: false, didSendViaMessagingTool: false, messagingToolSentTexts: [], messagingToolSentMediaUrls: [], messagingToolSentTargets: [], lastToolError: undefined, lastAssistant: { stopReason: "toolUse" }, itemLifecycle: { startedCount: 1, completedCount: 1, activeCount: 0 }, replayMetadata: { hadPotentialSideEffects: true, replaySafe: false }, toolMetas: [{ toolName: "bash", meta }] }); console.log("dummy sleep retry:", resolvePlanningOnlyRetryInstruction({ ...base, attempt: attempt("sleep 0.1") })?.includes("Act now") ?? false); console.log("ordinary bash retry:", resolvePlanningOnlyRetryInstruction({ ...base, attempt: attempt("pnpm test") }) ?? "null");'

  env -C "/home/0668001029/openclaw-worktrees/issue-64883" node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.incomplete-turn.test.ts
  env -C "/home/0668001029/openclaw-worktrees/issue-64883" corepack pnpm@11.2.2 tsgo:core
  env -C "/home/0668001029/openclaw-worktrees/issue-64883" corepack pnpm@11.2.2 tsgo:core:test
  env -C "/home/0668001029/openclaw-worktrees/issue-64883" corepack pnpm@11.2.2 exec oxlint --deny=warn --ignore-path=.oxlintignore src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/incomplete-turn.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.incomplete-turn.test.ts
  git -C "/home/0668001029/openclaw-worktrees/issue-64883" diff --check
  ```
- **Evidence after fix:**
  ```text
  Current main check:
  {
    "originMainStillBlocksToolUseStopReason": true,
    "originMainCountsEveryNonUpdatePlanToolAsProgress": true,
    "originMainHasSleepNoProgressHeuristic": false
  }

  Patched production helper proof:
  dummy sleep retry: true
  ordinary bash retry: null

  Focused Vitest:
  Test Files  1 passed (1)
       Tests  114 passed (114)
  [test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 22.30s

  Type/lint/whitespace:
  tsgo:core passed
  tsgo:core:test passed
  oxlint touched files passed
  git diff --check passed
  ```
- **Observed result after fix:** The same strict-agentic plan-only prose plus `sleep 0.1` now returns the act-now retry instruction, while a normal `pnpm test` bash tool-use turn still returns `null` and is not replayed as planning-only.
- **What was not tested:** No full embedded runner retry-exhaustion integration was added; this change is covered at the production helper seam recommended for runner decision logic.

## Regression Test Plan

- Coverage level: Focused helper regression test.
- Target test file: `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.incomplete-turn.test.ts`.
- Scenario locked in: strict-agentic `toolUse` turn with plan-only prose and `bash` meta `sleep 0.1` must return the planning-only retry instruction.
- Boundary guardrail: ordinary `bash` meta `pnpm test` remains non-retryable so real command activity is still treated as progress/side effect.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: `resolvePlanningOnlyRetryInstruction()` is the production decision point that the runner calls before retrying or surfacing the strict-agentic blocked state.

## Root Cause

- Root cause: planning-only detection counted every non-`update_plan` tool meta as progress and rejected any terminal stop reason other than `stop`, so a dummy sleep tool call with `stopReason: "toolUse"` skipped the text-based planning-only detector.
- Missing detection / guardrail: there was no no-progress tool classification for dummy delay commands before applying tool progress, replay-safety, item lifecycle, and `toolUse` terminal checks.
